ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: Duration in seconds before first distress cue
Rating of intensity of event for either a vocalization
Notes: 1 = Mild vocalizations that are not hedonically negative or positive (small noises, short babbles); 2 = Definite whimpering, Short duration; 3 = Longer whining/fussing/mild protest, rhythmic crying; 4 = Definite non-muted crying; 5 = Full intensity cry/scream

ElementDescription: Rating of intensity of event for expression
Notes: 0 = No facial; 111=N/A region shows codable fear movement.; 1 = Only one facial region shows codable movement; 2 = Only 2 facial regions show codable movement; 3 = An appearance change occurs in all 3 facial regions

ElementDescription: Time in seconds that reaching for the stimulus occurred (toy, spider, masks)
Time in seconds that attempts for self regulation occurred
Type of self regulation that occurred
Notes: 1 = Hand to mouth; 2 = Touching self; 3 = Reaching for mom; 4 = Table banging; 111=N/A
